hero.title: YouTube playlists -> videos
hero.description: A runnable example that loads collections from disk, then wires the workspace layout, sidebar nav, entity rail, entity workspace, entity cards, detail drawer, and selection helper together in host code.
hero.details: No API calls are hidden inside mpr-ui. This page fetches a local JSON file, picks a playlist, filters loaded videos, tracks selection, and swaps drawer content from plain browser code.

sections.label: Releases
sections.description: Near-term playlists with active review and launch work.

sections.label: Research
sections.description: Field notes, experiments, and reference cuts.

sections.label: Archive
sections.description: Evergreen back catalog and short-form clips.

playlists.title: Launch Queue
playlists.summary: Release candidates with caption timing and thumbnail QA already complete.
playlists.description: This playlist shows the default operational shape: concise rail tile, dense workspace rows, and a side drawer for the richer context that does not belong in the list itself.

playlists.title: Live Cuts
playlists.summary: Edited episodes with brand-safe callouts and sponsor reads.
playlists.description: A second release-oriented playlist keeps the rail horizontally scrollable and shows how the same shell can summarize a very different production flow.

playlists.title: Community Highlights
playlists.summary: Fast-turn community clips that pull comments and reactions into the edit.
playlists.description: The rail tile stays intentionally compact while the playlist drawer carries the narrative and the metric blocks.

playlists.title: Sponsor Reads
playlists.summary: Short sponsor segments with locked copy and legal review metadata.
playlists.description: A smaller tile still uses the same generic slots: title, meta, badge, and an action region that can carry app-owned meaning.

playlists.title: Field Notes
playlists.summary: Reference cuts, interview fragments, and raw observations for future stories.
playlists.description: This playlist is intentionally calmer than the release flows. It demonstrates that the same workspace chrome works for exploratory collections as well.

playlists.title: Archive Shorts
playlists.summary: Back-catalog short-form pieces that still perform well after resurfacing.
playlists.description: The archive lane proves the shell is still useful when the host data is quieter and the card actions are minimal.
